LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED WITH MODIFICATION ITS TENTATIVE RULING RE: DEFENDANTS SYDNEY LUSCUTOFF AND LUSCUTOFF, LENDORMY & ASSOCIATES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT TREATS DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION BROUGHT BY MONICA HUJAZI IN HER CAPACITY AS TRUSTEE AS IF IT WERE A MOTION TO STRIKE UNDER CCP 436. THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED WITH 60 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO RETAIN A LICENSED ATTORNEY TO REPRESENT HER INTERESTS AS TRUSTEE OF THE TRUST. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION FILED BY HUJAZI AS AN INDIVIDUAL ARE DENIED. THE FACE OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC") AND MATTERS JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE FAIL TO DISCLOSE THAT THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE TIME BARRED. PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLED CAUSATION AND THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NOT UNCERTAIN. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: LEO LAROCCA, ESQ., 415.981.5451, FOR PLAINTIFF MONICA HUJAZI, TRUSTEE OF THE ZUECHER FAMILY TRUST.
CAITLIN DIMAGGIO, ESQ., 415.788.1900, FOR DEFENDANTS SYDNEY LUSCUTOFF AND LUSCOTOFF, LENDORMY & ASSOCIATES.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted with modification its tentative ruling RE: DEFENDANTs SYDNEY LUSCUTOFF and LUSCUTOFF, LENDORMY & ASSOCIATES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court treats Defendants' demurrer to the first and second causes of action brought by Monica Hujazi in her capacity as trustee as if it were a motion to strike under CCP 436. The motion to strike is granted with 60 days leave to amend to retain a licensed attorney to represent her interests as trustee of the trust. The demurrer to the first and second causes of action filed by Hujazi as an individual are denied. The face of the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") and matters judicially noticeable fail to disclose that the causes of action are time barred. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled causation and the causes of action are not uncertain. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
